author: @cosmiikat-wav
text:
I know fuck and all about Morimens but that is not stopping me from ADORING THIS BANGER OH MY GODDDDDDDD.

Its simple, but it really works with it, the repeating "me" really hits the idea of desperately wishing to be remembered, that theyre hoping dearly for that request to be made.

The themes of spring combined with the idea of rotting flesh, crawling around inside her...her ideals rotting away and invading her very body? Contorting and twisting and snapping, as it becomes an amalgam of her...but not quite her, anymore? Im not so sure about this one.

Its a really pretty song, I dont think ive heard anything quite like it, but all music from this channel scratches the itch I have quite well, whatever it is, I very much enjoy it, this song sates a thirst indeed. 

Until next time I pop into a comments section for niche artists, ahah....

author: @AIcon1442
text: Alright, inspired by the music, I played the original *Song of Saya*. And I have a few... questions. Isaac, did you actually create two parts for your tracks because the original game also features two tracks titled "Song of Saya"—and because your songs share a similar mood with the original? The first part also struck me as quite a departure, given that you haven't released any instrumental tracks lately... Seriously? I’m simultaneously impressed and even slightly indignant at how I was led on.
